headline: What Verizon Communications (VZ)'s Dow Exit and Simpler Plans Strategy Means For Shareholders
summary: In June 2026, Verizon Communications completed cash tender and exchange offers covering about US$1.86 billion of subsidiary notes while also being removed from the Dow Jones Industrial Average, where Alphabet will take its place. Together with launching simplified Simplicity and Verizon One plans to address subscriber losses, these moves signal a focus on cleaner financing and more customer-centric wireless and broadband offerings. We’ll now examine how Verizon’s Dow exit and push into...

headline: 64 Years Old With $1.1 Million in a Traditional IRA. Here’s Where I’m Allocating Capital
summary: At 64, with $1.1 million in a Traditional IRA, tax-deferred cash flow is the point. With the 10-year Treasury at 4.50% and the 30-year at 4.94%, dividend yields have to earn their seat. The 10-year has swung between 4.43% and 4.56% in June alone, and that yield volatility is exactly what forced me to re-stack ... 64 Years Old With $1.1 Million in a Traditional IRA. Here’s Where I’m Allocating Capital

headline: Alphabet Set to Join Dow Jones Industrial Average as Verizon Exits Index (GOOG)
summary: Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) will replace Verizon (NYSE:VZ) in the Dow Jones Industrial Average, with the change scheduled to take effect on June 29, according to an announcement from S&P Dow Jones Indices. Investors responded positively to the news, with Alphabet shares rising 0.

headline: US stocks edge higher as falling oil prices help take pressure off the market
summary: Stocks edged higher on Wall Street Wednesday as falling bond yields and lower oil prices helped ease pressure on the market.  Technology stocks were mixed after two days of losses that heavily weighed on the broader market.  Micron Technology, which reports its latest financial results later Wednesday, fell 0.4% following its 13.2% plunge on Tuesday.

content.title: US stocks edge higher as falling oil prices help take pressure off the market
content.summary: Stocks edged higher on Wall Street Wednesday as falling bond yields and lower oil prices helped ease pressure on the market.  Technology stocks were mixed after two days of losses that heavily weighed on the broader market.  Micron Technology, which reports its latest financial results later Wednesday, fell 0.4% following its 13.2% plunge on Tuesday.
